reason: The recurring identity used here and throughout (I_A j = j = j I_B as maps B->A) follows purely from I_A, I_B being the MULTIPLICATIVE UNITS acting as the identity operator (I_A . j(y)=j(y); j(I_B . y)=j(y)). It does NOT use H4 unitality j(I_B)=I_A. The parenthetical 'by GT-hyp (H4) j(I_B)=I_A so j applied with the B-unit is consistent' is a muddled/spurious justification: H4 is never load-bearing in nodes 1.1-1.6. The derivation is CORRECT (proves the stronger H4-free statement), but the H4 citation should be removed/clarified so it does not imply unitality is needed. Non-blocking. Also worth a registry note: the contract advertises j(I)=I but the proof shows it is not required for intertwining theta.
